Back to Blog
March 7, 2026

Three More Quitman Cats Altered

Two spays and one neuter from the Quitman colony today — 18 cats altered at this location and counting. Same great partners, same mission.

Three more community cats from the Quitman colony were trapped, transported, and surgically altered today — two females spayed and one male neutered. Every surgery brings this colony closer to stabilization.

Spayed female — tortoiseshell with plenty of attitude

Spayed female — resting comfortably post-surgery

Neutered male — those green eyes say it all

Quitman Colony Progress

2

Females spayed today

1

Male neutered today

18

Total altered at this location

18 Down, More to Go

Just two weeks ago, we were celebrating 15 cats altered at this Quitman location. Today that number jumps to 18. What started as a handful of strays showing up at a homeowner's door has turned into one of our most active colony management sites in Brooks County.

The math hasn't changed: every unspayed female can produce up to three litters a year. These three cats alone could have been responsible for dozens of kittens by year's end. Now they won't be — and that's the whole point of TNR.

Same Team, Same Mission

Today's surgeries were once again made possible by our incredible partners. All Saints Cat TNR coordinated and sponsored the clinic, and Paws First Mobile Veterinary Clinic performed the surgeries on-site. These partnerships are the backbone of what we do — without them, cats like these three would still be adding to the population.

Today's Partners

🐱 All Saints Cat TNR

Coordinated and sponsored today's clinic.

allsaintscattnr.org

🏥 Paws First Mobile Veterinary Clinic

Provided on-site spay/neuter surgeries and veterinary care.

pawsfirstvet.com

You Can Help

Every surgery costs money — traps, transport, veterinary fees, and recovery supplies add up fast. Your donation directly funds the next cat's surgery and helps us keep this colony on the path to stabilization.

Want to support TNR in Brooks County? Every dollar goes directly toward spay/neuter surgeries for community cats.

Donate to Sam's TNR
